doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_set_zero: construct a signed-zero MPFR at the requested precision. Signed zero is observable in MPFR rounding (CLAUDE.md 'Signed zero is real'), so +0 and -0 are distinct return values — a port that drops sign on zero loses the rounding-edge-case observability. The C side accepts any int and treats non-negative as +; the TS surface restricts sign to 1|-1 strictly, throwing MPFRError(EPREC) on anything else so the contract is explicit at the call site.
divergence_from_c: C takes a pre-allocated mpfr_t (prec is implicit in the slot) and accepts any int as sign with 'non-negative → +0' coercion; TS takes prec as a positional bigint argument and a strict Sign (1|-1) — runtime sign=0 or other values throw MPFRError(EPREC) rather than silently producing +0. The strict shape forces callers to commit to a sign at the call site; the C silent-coerce-on-zero rule is the wrong default for an immutable-schema port.
refs: mpfr/src/set_zero.c L24–L30 — the C reference: mpfr_set_ui(x, 0, MPFR_RNDN); if (sign < 0) MPFR_SET_NEG(x).
src/core.ts L278–L292 — posZero / negZero constructors; both run the assertPrec bounds check.
src/core.ts L75–L90 — Sign discriminant; signed zero is observable.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts: Signed zero is real' — +0 and -0 are distinct MPFR values; the sign must survive through every op.
